mechanism: Compute correlations between measures of decision-making errors (violations of rational choice axioms) and a measure of general cognitive capacity. Examine whether correlations differ across classes of errors through stratified bivariate analysis. Identify subsets of error types that show significant association with capacity, and subsets that show none. Develop a predictive framework based on error and capacity characteristics.

Measure emotional and cognitive outcomes under a treatment condition (priming thoughts of an entity) and a control condition. Quantify differences in state-level psychological measures. Test whether individual differences in attachment dimensions moderate the effect of the treatment on choice of relationship target. Stratify analysis by attachment category.

Collect self-report measurements across an age range and stratify by age cohort. Compute item correlations within each domain, factor structure via dimensionality reduction, and inter-domain differentiation via correlation patterns. Quantify response consistency (acquiescence) within each age group. Compare psychometric properties across age groups and examine trends. Control for comprehension difficulty through item-level stratification.

Classify opinions as one of two categories based on a composite rule comparing opinion to group mean and group ideal. Measure willingness to express opinion and emotional states via questionnaire and behavioral observation. Test whether classification predicts expression tendency and emotional responses. Quantify effect sizes. Compare predictions across behavioral contexts.

Manipulate a social context (participant vs. observer role), measure preference or liking for entities, and compare across roles and decision conformity. Measure mediating variables (perception of implicit rejection). Test whether blocking the mediator eliminates the effect through conditional independence analysis. Analyze across distinct behavioral scenarios.

Induce a cognitive state (upward counterfactual thinking) and measure both contemporaneous emotional responses and subsequent behavioral measures (motivation, performance preparation, actual performance). Examine whether protective effects on self-esteem (via self-handicap framing) are associated with reduced motivation via correlational or conditional analysis. Test moderation by individual difference or contextual variables.

Measure intuitive beliefs about likelihood of negative outcomes following actions categorized as fate-tempting or non-fate-tempting. Test whether accessibility of negative outcomes mediates this belief. Measure negative outcome accessibility directly. Examine whether difficulty in attention redirection explains accessibility effects. Test whether effects persist under cognitive load or time pressure.

Measure expressed vulnerability (communication) and record beliefs about partner's perception of the entity's vulnerability. Measure doubts about partner authenticity and perceptions of rejection. Test a sequential mediation pathway: expressed vulnerability → perceived vulnerability in self → authenticity doubt → rejection perception → derogation and repeated vulnerability. Include experimental manipulation of perceived vulnerability to establish causal direction for late stages.

Synthesize results from longitudinal studies following entities through time, measuring change in two outcome variables (quality and stability). Organize findings by theoretical framework and extract common patterns across study designs. Identify variation by measurement method, time lag, and sample characteristics. Propose integrative model combining existing theories. Specify gaps and future research directions.

Conduct systematic literature search for associations between multiple distinct predictor variables (personality constructs) and multiple outcome variables (subjective well-being dimensions). Compute weighted average effect sizes using meta-analytic techniques. Stratify by outcome type and compare effect magnitudes across outcomes. Group predictors into higher-order categories and recompute estimates. Examine moderator variables through subgroup analysis.

Conduct meta-analytic synthesis of correlations between state and trait affective dimensions and multiple job-related outcome measures. Compute weighted effect sizes and confidence intervals. Test for unique contributions of each affective dimension to each outcome via multiple regression on effect sizes. Examine moderation by study characteristics and sample properties.
domain: organizational psychology, affect and job attitudes

mechanism: Propose that comparisons between a current stimulus and context-dependent reference points (norms) determine cognitive and emotional responses. Posit that norms are retrieved or constructed post-hoc by retrieving similar exemplars or generating counterfactuals rather than consulted from precomputed templates. Apply this principle to explain emotional response intensity, social inferences, and causal attribution. Develop formal activation model for norm construction.

Measure perceived predictability or likelihood of a research outcome after being told the outcome (treatment) versus before (control). Compute the difference or ratio of perceived likelihood across conditions. Test whether prompting consideration of alternative outcomes reduces the effect through group comparison. Examine whether lay observers' and experts' hindsight biases differ.

Measure subjective values assigned to stimuli that vary in a quantitative dimension (scope or magnitude). Classify stimuli or scenarios as affective (feeling-based) or calculative (deliberation-based) domains. Compare the functional form of value as a function of scope across domains via curve fitting or classification. Test whether the difference in scope sensitivity persists across diverse stimuli.

Manipulate the probability assigned to an event and measure how the entity represents the event cognitively and behaviorally via several response modes (categorization breadth, preference for description specificity, action segmentation, visual abstraction, detail identification, causality framing). Analyze whether low probability produces consistent shift toward abstract or high-level construals across measures. Test whether effects generalize across event types.

Induce a state of executive control exertion (attention, inhibition, working memory load, or emotion regulation) and measure subsequent executive capacity on a different task via a span, performance, or inhibition measure. Compare performance after control exertion versus control condition. Test whether effects are mediated by mood or motivation via measurement. Replicate across multiple control and outcome task pairs.

Measure self-reported emotional intensity for future events (anticipation) and past events (retrospection) of the same or parallel type. Compare mean intensity ratings across temporal direction. Measure metamemory or metamotional beliefs (expectations of future vs. remembered past intensity) and mental simulation frequency. Test whether simulation frequency mediates the anticipation-retrospection difference via conditional independence or regression analysis.

Introduce incidental difficulty or disfluency cues (sensory degradation, effortful processing) and measure whether reasoning task performance (heuristic bias, susceptibility to peripheral cues, syllogistic accuracy) improves. Analyze whether improvement reflects reduced reliance on System 1 heuristics and increased System 2 analytical processing. Test whether effects persist when difficulty is metacognitively attributed to task versus to processing ease.

Present sequences of discrete events spaced irregularly in time and measure recall of two attributes: order and timing. Analyze whether manipulations (grouping instructions) affect order and timing recall similarly. Compute correlations between input timing and recall timing. Test whether temporal crowding affects recall accuracy via stratified analysis. Propose models specifying what timing information contributes to order memory versus whether timing is independent.

Present sequences containing repeated items via rapid serial visual presentation and measure detection accuracy. Manipulate list length, explicit repetition expectancy, and presence of orthographically similar non-identical items. Test whether repetition advantage or blindness is observed via accuracy comparison. Analyze whether effects are driven by guessing strategy via conditional probability analysis. Control for comprehension level.
